jokes.text: I'm sorry, you're gonna have to give me the doggy bag. I couldn't make it.
jokes.setup: Jerry sets up the premise that asking for a doggy bag creates 'a sense of failure'
jokes.punchline: The apologetic, defeated tone when asking for the bag, like admitting personal failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor - takes a mundane social interaction and finds the underlying neurosis

jokes.text: Let me tell you, if you're a guy and you ask for the doggy bag on a date... you might as well have them just wrap up your genitals too. You're not gonna be needing those for a while either.
jokes.setup: Asking for a doggy bag on a date is emasculating
jokes.punchline: The escalation to wrapping up genitals - complete romantic failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mild embarrassment to total sexual failure. The specificity of 'wrap up your genitals' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Look at you. Look what you've become.
jokes.setup: Elaine rushing Jerry to hurry for her date
jokes.punchline: Jerry's mock-dramatic judgment of her behavior
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Jerry's tendency to judge, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: He'll like you more. - That's impossible.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests making Carl wait will make him like her more
jokes.punchline: Elaine's matter-of-fact response that it's impossible to like her more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine - confident to the point of absurdity, delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Stan, why do you have to pick your teeth at the table? - Leave me alone. - It's disgusting. Yeah, I'm gonna get married real soon.
jokes.setup: Couple arguing about disgusting table manners
jokes.punchline: The sarcastic prediction about marriage prospects given his behavior
jokes.explanation: Background humor that adds texture but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Guy's got quite a racket. I take you to dinner... then drop you at his apartment. And he gets the rest of my chicken.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing the unfairness of the dating situation
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about the leftover chicken being the final insult
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening bit, with the chicken being the specific indignity that makes it funny

jokes.text: Do I smell something? What am I, hard of smelling?
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if he smells something
jokes.punchline: 'Hard of smelling' - playing on 'hard of hearing'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that creates a new phrase while being immediately understandable

jokes.text: So when somebody has BO... the O usually stays with the B. Once the B leaves, the O goes with it.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to logic his way through the smell problem
jokes.punchline: Treating BO as if the letters are literally separable entities
jokes.explanation: Peak Seinfeld absurdist logic - taking an acronym literally to create pseudo-scientific reasoning

jokes.text: Let's see. I ski, I fish... I pillage, I plunder. - You pillage and plunder? - When I travel.
jokes.setup: Carl listing normal activities like skiing and fishing
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning pillaging and plundering as hobbies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to absurd, with the casual delivery making it funnier

jokes.text: Hey, what do you know? Look at that. A lesbian sighting. My lucky day.
jokes.setup: George spotting lesbians in the video store
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a rare wildlife sighting with boyish excitement
jokes.explanation: Captures George's immature fascination, though the concept is somewhat dated

jokes.text: They're so fascinating. Why is that? Because they don't want us. You gotta respect that.
jokes.setup: George wondering why lesbians are fascinating
jokes.punchline: Kramer's simple logic - they're appealing because they reject men
jokes.explanation: Kramer's backwards logic perfectly captures male psychology around rejection

jokes.text: Oh, boy. [George's reaction to seeing Susan with a woman]
jokes.setup: George recognizes his ex-girlfriend is now with a woman
jokes.punchline: His visible shock and discomfort
jokes.explanation: Sets up the uncomfortable encounter but isn't itself particularly funny

jokes.text: Come on, that's great. Are you kidding? I think that's fantastic. I've always encouraged experimentation. I'm the first guy in the pool.
jokes.setup: George trying to appear supportive of Susan's sexuality
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top enthusiasm revealing his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Classic George overcompensation - the more supportive he tries to sound, the more uncomfortable he reveals himself to be

jokes.text: Since you and I broke up. - So after me, you... went that way?
jokes.setup: Susan reveals the timing of her sexuality change
jokes.punchline: George's awkward phrasing 'went that way' like giving directions
jokes.explanation: The euphemistic language perfectly captures George's discomfort with direct discussion of sexuality

jokes.text: It's not even frontal nudity. It's 'sidal' nudity.
jokes.setup: George trying to downplay the adult content of his video rental
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'sidal' as if it's a legitimate film term
jokes.explanation: Invented terminology that sounds plausible enough to be real, showing George's attempt to intellectualize pornography

jokes.text: You didn't rewind it. There's a $2 charge for not rewinding. - But there's no signs here. - This is an outrage.
jokes.setup: Unexpected rewind charge at video store
jokes.punchline: George's theatrical outrage over a minor fee
jokes.explanation: Captures the petty irritations of 90s video rental culture and George's disproportionate reactions

jokes.text: I'm not giving you the satisfaction. I'm gonna watch it again.
jokes.setup: George refusing to pay the rewind fee
jokes.punchline: His 'punishment' is watching an erotic film again
jokes.explanation: Perfect George logic - turning what should be a hardship into a benefit while maintaining moral superiority

jokes.text: I figure by this time, the odor molecules have had at least 12 hours to 'de-smellify.'
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his scientific approach to the car smell
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'de-smellify' used as if it's technical terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld neologism - sounds scientific enough to be believable while being obviously made up

jokes.text: I can think of at least six known offensive odors that I would rather smell than what's living in your car. - What about skunk? - I don't mind skunk. - Horse manure? - I love horse manure.
jokes.setup: Elaine claiming there are worse smells than the car
jokes.punchline: Jerry's increasingly positive responses to objectively bad smells
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how bad the car smell must be by comparison to typically awful smells

jokes.text: When you're with a guy and he tells you he has to get up early... what does that mean? - It means he's lying.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about male behavior
jokes.punchline: Jerry's blunt, universal condemnation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation about dating behavior delivered with Jerry's matter-of-fact certainty

jokes.text: Jerry, I'm sure I've seen men on the street early in the morning. - Well, sometimes we do have to get up early... but a man will always trade sleep for sex.
jokes.setup: Elaine challenging Jerry's generalization about men
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pseudo-scientific rule about male priorities
jokes.explanation: Jerry presenting his theory as universal law, typical of his character's confidence in his observations

jokes.text: Is it possible I'm not as attractive as I think I am? - Anything's possible.
jokes.setup: Elaine's vulnerable self-doubt
jokes.punchline: Jerry's unhelpfully honest response
jokes.explanation: Jerry's inability to provide comfort when friendship calls for it, staying true to his self-centered nature

jokes.text: I know the list.
jokes.setup: George rattling off Yankees prospects Steinbrenner traded away
jokes.punchline: Jerry's weary acknowledgment that this is a familiar rant
jokes.explanation: Captures the repetitive nature of George's Yankees complaints and Jerry's long-suffering friendship

jokes.text: You stink. - What do you mean, I stink? - You stink.
jokes.setup: George asking what Jerry means
jokes.punchline: Jerry's repetition without elaboration
jokes.explanation: Simple repetition joke that works through Jerry's bluntness but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: It's attached itself to me. It's alive.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing the smell has transferred from car to person
jokes.punchline: Treating the odor as a living entity that can attach itself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the smell from problem to supernatural entity, setting up the episode's central metaphor

jokes.text: So let me ask you, do you think I could've done this? - No, no. It's the valet guy. - No, no. I mean, driven Susan to lesbianism.
jokes.setup: George asking if he could have caused something
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he's talking about Susan's sexuality, not the car smell
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that perfectly captures George's narcissistic tendency to make everything about himself

jokes.text: Suicide, maybe. Not lesbianism.
jokes.setup: George wondering if he drove Susan to lesbianism
jokes.punchline: Jerry suggesting suicide would be more likely than sexual orientation change
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economical - suggests George is so terrible he could drive someone to suicide but not change their sexual orientation

jokes.text: Oh, this isn't even BO. This is beyond BO. It's BBO.
jokes.setup: George experiencing the full impact of the smell
jokes.punchline: Creating a new category beyond BO - 'BBO' (Beyond Body Odor)
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym that builds on the episode's letter-based humor while escalating the smell problem

jokes.text: There should be a BO squad that patrols the city... to sniff them out, strip them down and wash them with a soapy brush.
jokes.setup: George's frustration with body odor in general
jokes.punchline: The dystopian vision of a city hygiene police force
jokes.explanation: George's typically extreme solution to a social problem, escalating to absurd authoritarian measures

jokes.text: The funny thing is, somehow I find her more appealing now. Like, if I knew she was a lesbian when we went out... I never would've broken up with her.
jokes.setup: George reflecting on Susan's new sexuality
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic that he'd want her more when she's unavailable
jokes.explanation: Perfect George psychology - wanting what he can't have, with the illogical thinking that defines his character

jokes.text: Maybe you stunk up the car, rather than the car stinking up you. - Oh, it's the chicken and the egg.
jokes.setup: Manager suggesting Jerry caused the smell
jokes.punchline: Jerry treating it as a philosophical paradox
jokes.explanation: Jerry applying a classic paradox to a mundane situation, showing his tendency to intellectualize everything

jokes.text: Yes! Yes, 125 is good. Now could you please open the door! Let me out of here!
jokes.setup: Manager trapped in the smelly car during negotiations
jokes.punchline: His desperation to escape overriding business sense
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy where the smell becomes a weapon, forcing quick capitulation

jokes.text: Someone stole Rochelle, Rochelle? - Well, you left the window open. - We had to air out the car.
jokes.setup: Discovery that the erotic video was stolen from the car
jokes.punchline: The necessity of airing out the car led to the theft
jokes.explanation: The smell problem creates a new problem, showing how one issue cascades into others

jokes.text: The O went from the valet's B to the car to me.
jokes.setup: George explaining the smell transfer
jokes.punchline: Using Jerry's B and O theory as scientific fact
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how Jerry's absurd theory has been adopted as truth by George

jokes.text: It clings to everything. Jerry thinks it's an entity.
jokes.setup: George describing the smell's properties
jokes.punchline: Escalating it to supernatural status as an 'entity'
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of the smell from problem to living force

jokes.text: But I showered, and I shampooed. So... - That's a relief. - Yeah. - What? - It's still there.
jokes.setup: George confident he's solved his smell problem
jokes.punchline: Elaine's immediate detection that he hasn't
jokes.explanation: Classic setup and deflation - George's confidence immediately undermined by reality

jokes.text: I need, like, 35. Thanks. - So was it me? - Oh, don't be ridiculous. Is that what you wanted to talk to me about?
jokes.setup: George borrowing money from Susan
jokes.punchline: His real agenda being to ask about causing her lesbianism
jokes.explanation: Shows George's inability to separate his personal neuroses from normal social interactions

jokes.text: Let me ask you something: If you and Mona were ever to dance... how do you decide who leads? I mean, do you take turns? Do you discuss it beforehand? How does that work?
jokes.setup: George trying to understand lesbian relationship dynamics
jokes.punchline: His awkward, overly detailed questions about dancing
jokes.explanation: George's social awkwardness and inappropriate curiosity about lesbian relationships

jokes.text: You're an idiot. - Why? That's a legitimate sociological question.
jokes.setup: George's inappropriate questions about lesbian relationships
jokes.punchline: His defense that he's being scientific
jokes.explanation: George's typical response to being called out - claiming intellectual high ground

jokes.text: Oh, and George, by the way... you stink. You need a bath. - It's not me! It's the car!
jokes.setup: Susan delivering a final insult as she leaves
jokes.punchline: George's desperate, futile protest about the source
jokes.explanation: George's humiliation is complete - even his ex-girlfriend who turned lesbian finds him repulsive

jokes.text: Even Superman would be helpless against this kind of stench.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing the power of the smell
jokes.punchline: Invoking Superman as being no match for it
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that positions the smell as more powerful than the ultimate superhero

jokes.text: This isn't just an odor. You need a priest to get rid of this thing.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why Kramer shouldn't buy the car
jokes.punchline: Treating the smell like a demonic possession requiring exorcism
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation of the smell metaphor - from problem to supernatural entity requiring religious intervention

jokes.text: It's a presence. It's the beast.
jokes.setup: Continuing the supernatural characterization of the smell
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'the beast' like a horror movie monster
jokes.explanation: Completes the transformation of a bad smell into biblical/horror movie terminology

jokes.text: Amazing. I drive them to lesbianism, he brings them back.
jokes.setup: Learning that Kramer has converted Susan's girlfriend back to heterosexuality
jokes.punchline: George seeing himself as having the opposite effect of Kramer on women's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of George vs. Kramer's effect on women. Perfect economy and character insight. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm Kramer.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning how Kramer converted a lifelong lesbian
jokes.punchline: Kramer's simple, confident explanation of his appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - supreme confidence and self-awareness distilled into two words. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: You happen to be a very eligible lesbian.
jokes.setup: George comforting Susan about dating prospects
jokes.punchline: Treating lesbianism like a dating demographic with market qualifications
jokes.explanation: George applying dating advice language to lesbian relationships in his typically tone-deaf way

jokes.text: I can't sell this car. This thing. It's got to be stopped.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing the smell has spread to his jacket
jokes.punchline: Treating the car like a threat to humanity that must be destroyed
jokes.explanation: Jerry taking moral responsibility to prevent the spread of the smell - heroic language for mundane problem

jokes.text: So, what do you want to do? - Sauce me.
jokes.setup: George asking what to do about his persistent smell
jokes.punchline: Jerry's reference to the tomato sauce treatment mentioned earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier scene with economical phrasing that sounds both desperate and absurd

jokes.text: Why do we need BO? What is the function of it? Everything in nature has a reason, has a purpose, except BO.
jokes.setup: Jerry's closing monologue about body odor
jokes.punchline: Questioning the evolutionary purpose of something universally unpleasant
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld stand-up premise - questioning the logic behind universal human experiences

jokes.text: You move, you stink. Why can't our bodies help us? Why can't sweat smell good?
jokes.setup: Jerry noting the counterproductive nature of exercise creating bad smells
jokes.punchline: Imagining if evolution had made sweat pleasant
jokes.explanation: Continues the observational premise with logical follow-up questions

jokes.text: Instead of putting your laundry in the hamper, put it in a vase.
jokes.setup: Jerry imagining a world where sweat smells good
jokes.punchline: The specific image of displaying dirty laundry as decoration
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual image that perfectly inverts our normal relationship with dirty clothing

jokes.text: Probably have a dirty sock hanging from the rearview mirror of your car.
jokes.setup: Continuing the alternate universe where body odor smells good
jokes.punchline: Replacing air fresheners with dirty socks
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the episode's car smell theme while extending the absurd premise

jokes.text: Then on a special night, maybe a little underwear... coming out of your breast pocket. Just to show her that she's important.
jokes.setup: Jerry continuing his alternate universe scenario
jokes.punchline: Using dirty underwear as a romantic gesture like a corsage or handkerchief
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that combines romantic gesture with gross-out humor in a completely original way
